Organism: Mentha piperita (Peppermint) (Mentha aquatica x Mentha spicata)
Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family, COMT subfamily
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. COMT subfamily. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=3,3',4',5,7,8-hexahydroxyflavone + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 3,3',4',5,7-pentahydroxy-8-methoxyflavone + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:16593, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:58544, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:77862; EC=2.1.1.88; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:14697269}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=4',7,8-trihydroxyflavone + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 4',7-dihydroxy-8-methoxyflavone + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:73135, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:192709, ChEBI:CHEBI:192765; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:14697269}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:73136; Evidence={ECO:0000305|PubMed:14697269}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=8-hydroxy-7-methoxyflavone + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 7,8-dimethoxyflavone + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:73131, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:192766, ChEBI:CHEBI:192767; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:14697269}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:73132; Evidence={ECO:0000305|PubMed:14697269};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Flavonoid 8-O-methyltransferase involved in the biosynthesis of polymethoxylated flavonoids natural products such as pebrellin, aroma compounds which contribute to the flavor of peppermint, and exhibit pharmacological activities such as anti-allergic, anti-oxidant, antibacterial, anti-proliferative, and anti-inflammatory effects (PubMed:14697269). Catalyzes S-adenosylmethionine-dependent regioselective 8-O-methylation of flavonoids; active on various hydroxylated flavonoid substrates, including 7,8,3'4'-tetrahydroxy-flavone, 7,8,4'-trihydroxy-flavone and 8-hydroxy-flavone 7-methyl ether (PubMed:14697269). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:14697269}.
